provenance: Hand-authored fictional record. NOT a real person. No PHI. Created for schema/example purposes in the Preventive Health Model Lab.

demographics.notes: Office worker, sedentary job. Values invented for illustration.

medications.name: atorvastatin 10mg
medications.note: started after LDL trend + family CAD; SYNTHETIC

lifestyle.activity: sedentary; ~3000 steps/day self-reported; began light walking Q2-2025
lifestyle.diet: self-reports frequent takeout, sugary drinks 1-2/day through 2024, cutting back in 2025

timeline.note: Baseline. Slightly overweight. Lipids borderline; fasting glucose normal.
Repeat lipid panel; LDL creeping up.
HbA1c now in prediabetes range (5.7-6.4). Weight up 2kg. BP trending up. Counseled on diet/activity.
Fasting glucose still elevated. Patient reports minimal lifestyle change so far.
HbA1c 6.3% (upper prediabetes). BP now elevated/stage-1 range on single reading. Started atorvastatin 10mg given LDL trend + strong family CAD history. Discussed structured lifestyle program.
Early response: began walking + cut sugary drinks. HbA1c down slightly to 6.1%; LDL improved on statin. Encouraged to continue; recheck in 6 months.

notes: SYNTHETIC record for pipeline/example use only. Trends chosen to illustrate a preventive-health reasoning scenario (rising HbA1c into prediabetes, LDL creep, BP creep, strong family cardiometabolic history). No claim of clinical accuracy for any real individual.
